text: The surgeon divides the vein using surgical instruments, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon prepares to address the urethra following the division of the vein, indicating the next step in the prostatectomy procedure.
The surgeon visually identifies the catheter as it becomes visible in the surgical field during the prostatectomy procedure.
The surgeon repositions the catheter, which is visible within the surgical field.
The surgeon divides the ureter using surgical instruments, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon exposes the prostate, revealing its anatomical position in the surgical field.
The surgeon places the excised tissue into a specimen bag and moves it aside to clear the surgical field.
